title: Scenario-stratified flagship-v1 vs REF-C-base closed-loop (on NuRec reconstructions) — MEASURED 2026-07-23
label: on NuRec reconstructions (AlpaSim, tanitad-eval A40), 480x854
framing_MANDATORY: WITHIN-SIM RELATIVE, not absolute. Both models see the SAME ~3.2x-OOD NuRec reconstruction input (RUN_RECIPE s13); the per-category flagship-vs-REF-C RANKING is trustworthy, the absolute rates are NOT real-world.

labeling.method: keyframe visual classification by direct multimodal inspection (one mid-clip frame per scene, camera_front_wide_120fov.mp4 @ t=2s, 640px). No scene-type descriptor field exists in the NuRec/public_2601 metadata (checked: only a telemetry CSV; USDZ carries calib not semantics). Cosmos-Reason1-7B IS cached on the pod but needs a separate transformers/qwen2.5-vl env build (deferred); direct inspection is equivalent for this n and fully auditable (keyframes staged).
caveats: a single mid-clip frame can miss a light/junction the scene traverses later
traffic-light and roundabout cannot be confirmed from one frame — none seen here

scale_target_vs_achieved: TARGET was ~15-25 scenes/category (75-125 total). ACHIEVED = the existing 12-scene suite, which is far too small AND skewed to populate 5 categories (3 categories represented; roundabout + traffic-light absent). This CONFIRMS the coordinator's premise that 12 is too few. Scaling to target = ~75-125 new scene downloads (~1.5 GB USDZ each) + closed-loop runs for BOTH models (multi-hour); the download+keyframe+classify pipeline is staged (kf_download.sh, Xet-disabled) — ESCALATED as a separate compute budget.

highway.read: TIE. Identical outcomes scene-by-scene: both PASS 0009402a, both COLLIDE at-fault on 00097de1, both OFFROAD on 000e95f7. mean score 0.106 vs 0.141 (within noise). Flagship is NOT worse on highways — consistent with the n=1 highway scene (01d503d4) where flagship even beat REF-C.

intersection.read: REF-C wins the single intersection (downtown, score 1.0 vs flagship offroad 0.0). n=1 — directional only.

straight/other.read: REF-C DOMINATES urban/rural straight roads: pass 6/8 vs 1/8, mean score 0.566 vs 0.060, and flagship goes OFFROAD 6/8 (vs REF-C 1/8). This is where flagship's entire deficit lives.

headline: Flagship v1's closed-loop deficit vs REF-C is NOT uniform — it is CONCENTRATED on straight/urban/rural roads (pass 1/8 vs 6/8; flagship offroad 6/8), while on HIGHWAY the two TIE (identical 1/3 outcomes). Flagship's wide-swerve WM+tactical policy survives wide highway lanes but drives off narrower urban roads. The aggregate '-0.30 to -0.43 score delta' is really 'REF-C wins decisively off-highway, ties on-highway'. Actionable: flagship's road-keeping on non-highway geometry is the target, not its highway behaviour.

provenance.closed_loop_source: flagship_vs_refc_suite_results.json (480x854, one rollout/scene, canon f_eff 265.6/265.7 OK; per-scene rollout UUIDs there).
provenance.native_1080_cross_check: the native re-run (flagship_vs_refc_native1080_results.json) shows the same category pattern qualitatively (highway closer, straight/urban REF-C-favored).

caveats: n=12, thin per category (highway 3, intersection 1, straight 8) — DIRECTIONAL not powered
roundabout + traffic-light categories have ZERO scenes — unmeasured
